# gelatops

> All-in-one management platform for artisan gelato and ice cream businesses — recipes, production planning, food costs, EU allergen labelling, stockpile tracking and online-order intake in one web app.

gelatops is built for artisan gelato makers, gelaterie, small ice-cream production labs and pastry kitchens. It centralises recipe management, real-time food costing at 1 L / 5 L / 10 L / 15 L, EU 1169/2011 allergen tracking with sub-recipe inheritance, production planning with frozen-snapshot shopping lists, supplier auto-ordering, and Stripe-webhook online-order intake. The product replaces the spreadsheets, Word documents, and phone calls that most gelaterias currently rely on.

## Key facts

- gelatops covers all 14 mandatory allergen categories under EU Regulation 1169/2011.
- Production plans freeze ingredient prices at submission, so margins are locked the moment you commit.
- gelatops integrates with any online shop that can fire a Stripe webhook — including Stripe Checkout, custom Stripe-based shops, and WooCommerce / Shopify when configured to emit webhook events.
- A free Starter plan is available, and a free trial can be arranged via hello@gelatops.com.
- Pricing is tiered: Starter (free, up to 10 recipes), Studio (unlimited recipes plus production planning, allergen tracking and stockpile), Pro (multi-shop integration plus supplier auto-ordering and smart recipe balancing). Final prices are tailored to the size of the business.
- The recipe system is ingredient-agnostic: gelato, sorbet, vegan, milk-based and fruit-based recipes all use the same tooling.
- Recipes can include sub-recipes; allergens declared on a sub-recipe automatically propagate to the parent.
- gelatops runs on AWS with encrypted connections, automated database backups, and private infrastructure.
- The product is web-only and runs in any modern browser; there is nothing to install.
- The interface is available in English, Italian and German.

## How it works (4 steps)

1. Set up your recipes — add gelato and sorbet recipes with per-litre ingredient amounts and ingredient prices.
2. Add what you need to produce — items enter the production queue manually, or land automatically via Stripe webhook from your online shop.
3. Build your production plan — assign queue items to dated production runs, adjust quantities, review the cost summary.
4. Submit and order — get a frozen shopping list with quantities and prices locked; print, export, or auto-send to a connected supplier.
